- [x] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.
- [x] Clarify Project Requirements - Requirements: Svelte-Vite framework, Cesium for 3D earth globe, TypeScript, writable stores, interactive zoom/rotate
- [x] Scaffold the Project - Created complete Svelte-TypeScript project structure with Vite
- [x] Customize the Project - Integrated Cesium 3D globe with proper TypeScript support and Svelte stores
- [x] Install Required Extensions - No extensions needed for this Vite project
- [x] Compile the Project - Dependencies installed, TypeScript errors resolved, build successful
- [x] Create and Run Task - Development server task created and running on http://localhost:5173/
- [x] Launch the Project - Application launched in browser
- [x] Ensure Documentation is Complete - README.md created with comprehensive setup and usage instructions

Project successfully created! The Svelte Globe App is now ready with:
- Interactive 3D Earth globe using Cesium
- TypeScript for type safety
- Svelte stores for state management (globeStore)
- Real-time camera position tracking
- Responsive design with info panels

To get started:
1. Get a Cesium Ion access token from https://ion.cesium.com/
2. Replace the placeholder token in src/lib/GlobeViewer.svelte
3. The dev server is already running at http://localhost:5173/
